title: coccoloba nervosa subsp. cristalensis
intro: Coccoloba nervosa subsp. cristalensis, also known as the cristal palm tree, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Polygonaceae and is native to Cuba. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The cristal palm tree is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its leaves are also used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, the leaves of Coccoloba nervosa subsp. cristalensis are often prepared as a tea or infusion, and the plant is also used in herbal remedies to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.
